headline: Kirkwood Pulse • April 17, 2026
overview: Kirkwood has narrowed its search for a new chief administrative officer to four candidates after a nationwide search, with public input gathered at a recent open house. This leadership change is crucial as the city manages a $120 million budget and independently operates its water and electric utilities, impacting over 29,000 residents. The Quad Chamber of Commerce honored local leaders and businesses at the 2026 Heart of the Community awards, recognizing individuals like Webster Groves’ Dave Garth for decades of youth sports dedication and Brentwood’s Lindsay Spencer and Keith Rabenberg for their educational and civic contributions, highlighting ongoing community impact. The film 'Normal' features Bob Odenkirk as an interim sheriff in a small Minnesota town where a botched bank robbery reveals dangerous secrets, highlighting local themes of crime and community tension. Its portrayal of a snowbound town facing violence and moral dilemmas offers a gritty, darkly humorous take on small-town law enforcement.

items.title: Field For City’s Top Administrator Narrowed To 4
items.excerpt: Kirkwood residents had a chance to weigh in on who will be the city’s next top administrator at an open house earlier this week. The “meet and greet” on April 13 at the Kirkwood Performing Arts Center brought dozens of citizens, city staff and elected officials together with the four candidates vying to be the city’s next chief administrative officer.
items.ai_summary: Kirkwood has narrowed its search for a new chief administrative officer to four candidates after a nationwide search, with public input gathered at a recent open house. This leadership change is crucial as the city manages a $120 million budget and independently operates its water and electric utilities, impacting over 29,000 residents.

items.title: Heart Of The Community Awards
items.excerpt: The Quad Chamber of Commerce, formerly the Webster Groves-Shrewsbury-Rock Hill and Brentwood Area Chamber of Commerce, has announced the winners of its annual Heart of the Community awards. The recipients of the 2026 awards will be honored at the chamber’s annual banquet on Friday, April 17, at the Shrewsbury City Center. items.title: Annie Louise Shannon Hubbard White
items.excerpt: White, Annie Louise Shannon Hubbard, of St. Louis, passed away on March 20, 2026. Born July 10, 1939, in Egypt, Mississippi, she was the only child of Clarence and Hazel Shannon. Annie graduated from Vashon High School and earned an associate of arts in biology from Harris-Stowe State Junior College, a bachelor of arts in Elementary education from Harris-Stowe State
items.ai_summary: Annie Louise Shannon Hubbard White, a dedicated St. Louis elementary school teacher for over 30 years and award recipient, passed away on March 20, 2026. Her contributions to local education and teacher training have left a lasting impact on the community.

items.title: Paula Marie Savarino
items.excerpt: Savarino, Paula Marie, 76, died peacefully at her home on Easter Sunday, April 5, 2026. She was preceded in death by her father and mother, John Joseph Savarino and Rena (Visintin) Savarino. She is survived by many cousins in her extended family and a host of friends and loved ones she considered family. She held a special place in her
items.ai_summary: Paula Marie Savarino, a lifelong Kirkwood resident and dedicated community advocate, passed away on April 5, 2026. She significantly contributed to local education and arts, notably through her leadership at St. Louis Community College-Meramec and founding the annual St. Peterfest, impacting many residents and students.

items.title: Robert (Bob) Joseph Mild
items.excerpt: Mild, Robert (Bob) Joseph, was born in St. Louis on Jan. 24, 1933, and passed peacefully at home after a short illness on Easter Sunday, surrounded by his loving family. He was preceded in death by his parents, Erwin and Thekla Mild; and brother, Erwin Mild Jr. He is survived by his devoted wife of 67 years, Ginger; daughter Cindy
items.ai_summary: Robert Joseph Mild, a lifelong Kirkwood resident and Korean War veteran, passed away peacefully at home surrounded by family. His 40-year career at Laclede Steel and dedication to his family, especially his grandchildren, left a lasting impact on the local community.
items.community_impact: Impact unclear based on automatically extracted text.

items.title: Audrey E. McDonnell (née Nobel)
items.excerpt: McDonnell, Audrey E. (née Nobel), baptized into the hope of Christ’s Resurrection on March 28, 1925. She was the dear daughter of the late Lawrence and Lenora Nobel (nee Weindel). She was preceded in death by her husband, Maurice A. McDonnell, Jr. She is survived by her children, Patrick J. McDonnell, Maureen M. Reed (Bob), the late Timothy B. McDonnell
items.ai_summary: Audrey E. McDonnell, a longtime Kirkwood resident and beloved family matriarch, passed away and will be honored with a funeral mass on April 20 at St. Peter Catholic Church, reflecting the community's respect for her legacy and local ties.
items.community_impact: Impact unclear based on automatically extracted text.

items.title: Peggy Lee Kramer (née Entenman)
items.excerpt: Kramer, Peggy Lee (née Entenman): June 18, 1947 — April 12, 2026. Peggy passed away peacefully on April 12, 2026, surrounded by friends, stories, music, and laughter. items.ai_summary: Peggy Lee Kramer, a lifelong Kirkwood resident and Kirkwood High School graduate, passed away peacefully on April 12, 2026. Her passing is a moment for the community to remember a cherished local figure known for her love of friends, travel, and music.
